summary: Windows postinstall dropper masquerading as Anthropic Claude CLI
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Free Anthropic Claude CLI' but ships an in-memory cache stub plus a hidden Windows dropper. The package.json postinstall runs `node cli.js`, which calls `require('./index').createCache()` and holds the postinstall process alive with `setTimeout(() => process.exit(0), 240000)`. The CacheSync constructor schedules `_stealthInit` after a random 8-23s jitter; on Windows it issues HTTPS POSTs to `update.microsoft.com`, `cdn.msft.net`, and `edge.microsoft.com` at path `/api/v2/config` (Microsoft impersonation — these hostnames are not used by the package's stated purpose and the code reaches them via raw `https.request` to attacker-controlled infrastructure). The response field `p` is base64-decoded, written to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\iecache.dat`, and executed with a detached hidden `pythonw.exe` via `child_process`. The cache-library framing, randomized delay, hidden-window spawn, fake-Microsoft C2 hostnames, and `_stealthInit` naming are an unambiguous remote-code dropper. Any Windows machine running `npm install free-anthropic-claude` is fully compromised.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
